name: The Sun
fact: The Sun contains 99.86% of all mass in the solar system. Its gravity holds everything — from Mercury to the Oort Cloud — in orbit. Every second, it fuses 600 million tonnes of hydrogen into helium, releasing the energy that has sustained life on Earth for 4.5 billion years.
bio: A 4.6-billion-year-old G-type main-sequence star, halfway through its 10-billion-year life. The Sun rotates differentially — its equator takes 25 days, its poles 35. In about 5 billion years it will exhaust its core hydrogen, expand into a red giant, and engulf the inner planets. The Sun's gravitational influence defines the heliosphere out to roughly 120 AU.
